name: PR-CV 212 Penya Migjorn Trail
name_original: Ascenso al Penya Migjorn (PR-CV 212) desde Xixona
description: Hike the Ascent to Penya Migjorn (PR-CV 212) from Xixona, a moderate 7.3-mile route with 2416 feet of gain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Xixona / Jijona offers a diverse landscape characterized by Mediterranean, often dry and rocky, terrain. The region features prominent mountain peaks, including Penya Migjorn and Alt de la Martina, which provide extensive panoramic views. Pine and holm oak trees adorn the slopes, and several routes explore canyons carved by the Montnegre River. This varied topography provides a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Xixona / Jijona The most popular hiking route is PR-CV 212 Penya Migjorn Trail, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route ascends Penya Migjorn, offering panoramic views of the surrounding area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Penya Migjorn Peak – Geodesic Vertex loop from Casa del Butaner, a moderate 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This loop provides views of the Penya Migjorn peak and the Mediterranean landscape. Local hikers also love the Carrasqueta Pass – Els Plans Summit loop from Serra de la Carrasqueta, an 8.4 miles (13.6 km) trail leading through the Serra de la Carrasqueta, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Xixona / Jijona is defined by rocky Mediterranean terrain, pine forests, and mountain peaks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Xixona / Jijona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Xixona / Jijona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Xixona / Jijona?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes in Xixona / Jijona, catering to various skill levels. Most of these, around 20, are considered moderate, with one easy and one difficult option also available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Xixona / Jijona?
A: The terrain in Xixona / Jijona is typically Mediterranean, characterized by dry, rocky landscapes, adorned with pine and holm oak trees. You'll encounter prominent mountain peaks, deep canyons carved by the Montnegre River, and areas offering wide-ranging views of the coast and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, there is at least one easy hiking option available. For a pleasant natural environment, the area around Barranc de les Salines is known for its accessibility. Always check individual route details for suitability for families.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Hikers can explore several impressive natural features. The majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2835932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maigmó Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2604644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning Mediterranean landscapes. For panoramic views, consider reaching the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2874564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante Balcony&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter historical elements like ancient snow wells or the Tibi Dam.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Xixona / Jijona?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27632108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Migjorn Peak – Geodesic Vertex loop from Casa del Butaner&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.6 km path. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14661392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass – Els Plans Summit loop from Serra de la Carrasqueta&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.6 km long.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Xixona / Jijona?
A: The hiking trails in Xixona / Jijona are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from nearly 1000 reviews. Over 5000 hikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the panoramic views from peaks like Penya Migjorn and the diverse Mediterranean landscapes.

Q: Which mountains can I climb for panoramic views?
A: Xixona / Jijona offers several peaks with rewarding panoramic views. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e863374198/pr-cv-212-penya-migjorn-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Migjorn (1223m)&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent choice, offering extensive vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14660693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de la Martina (1093m)&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent views of Jijona and the surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any trails that explore canyons or river areas?
A: Yes, some routes delve into the beautiful canyons carved by the waters of the Montnegre River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14634602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Terraces along the Montnegre – Barranco de Les Salines loop from granja&lt;/a&gt; is one such trail that explores this unique sculpted relief and varied rock colors.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Xixona / Jijona?
A: Given the Mediterranean climate, spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in Xixona / Jijona, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be hot and dry, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winters are typically mild, though higher elevations might experience cooler conditions.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many popular hiking areas and starting points in Xixona / Jijona, especially those leading to well-known peaks or natural parks, typically have designated or informal parking options. It's advisable to check specific route information for precise parking locations.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails using public transport?
A: Public transport options to reach specific trailheads directly might be limited in some of the more remote areas of Xixona / Jijona. However, the town of Xixona / Jijona itself is accessible by public transport, and from there, local services or a short taxi ride might connect you to closer starting points for certain routes. Planning ahead is recommended.
tips: The climb to Penya Migjorn is demanding and requires being prepared for it with appropriate footwear, walking sticks (recommended) and plenty of water, since the physical effort is important and there will be no place to recharge along the way. The reward will be the beautiful views from its summit at more than 1,220 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: Where does the Ascent to Penya Migjorn (PR-CV 212) from Xixona trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Xixona. While specific parking details aren't provided, Xixona is a town, so parking options should be available. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally rated as Medium/High or Moderate-High. It demands a good level of physical fitness and experience in mountain hiking due to significant elevation gain and challenging sections, including minor scrambles near the summit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. It begins with paved sections, dirt tracks, and paths through fields. As it enters the Barranco del Choquero, it becomes a winding ascent. The latter stages are demanding, with steep, rocky sections and loose stones. Near the peak, there's an exposed stretch requiring light scrambling where hands are needed for assistance, and some parts involve less defined paths over rock platforms.

faqs.question: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to magnificent views from Xixona's castle at the start. The route leads towards the impressive vertical walls of Penya Migjorn, also known as Penya Roja due to its reddish limestone. You might observe the 'Forat de la Penya,' a large, shallow cave. The summit, known as Penya Migjorn Peak, and various points during the ascent offer spectacular panoramic vistas of surrounding mountains and the Mediterranean coast. The descent passes by the Cova dels Corrals and its cistern.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Due to limited vegetation and sun exposure, it is highly recommended to avoid hiking on hot days. Spring and autumn are generally the best times to visit, offering more moderate temperatures for this strenuous hike.

faqs.question: Is the Ascent to Penya Migjorn (PR-CV 212) from Xixona dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail's difficulty, including steep, rocky sections and minor scrambles, along with exposed areas, might make it challenging for some dogs. While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, consider your dog's fitness and agility before bringing them on this demanding route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required for hiking the PR-CV 212 to Penya Migjorn from Xixona.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The PR-CV 212 is described as having a partially circular nature, allowing for a varied return, for example, via sites like Cova dels Corrals. This suggests it's not a simple out-and-back.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The area is known for Griffon Vultures, which can often be seen soaring overhead, adding to the scenic experience.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty, sun exposure, and duration, you should carry ample water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), appropriate hiking footwear with good grip for rocky sections, and possibly gloves for the scrambling parts. Snacks and a first-aid kit are also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or sections I should be aware of on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the most challenging aspect is the exposed section near the summit, which involves minor scrambles ('trepadas') where hands are needed for assistance, and there is a distinct sensation of verticality. Caution is paramount in these areas as a fall could have serious consequences. Also, be mindful of loose stones on steep, rocky sections.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, Carrer de Sant Vicent, and Carrer de les Parres.
